summary: RawFit Gym is a full-stack gym management web application (DFINITY Internet Computer backend in Motoko + React/Tailwind frontend) that supports admin operations (member registration, diet/workout charts, membership packages, attendance management, and video uploads) and member-facing experiences (personal dashboard with assigned plans, attendance history, QR-based check-in/out, and a location-gated video library). The system uses role-based access control (admin/user/guest), Internet Identity for admin authentication, a separate membership-ID based member login flow on the frontend, secure gym QR code generation/validation, and blob storage integration for video uploads.
architectural_notes: Frontend uses React with TanStack React Query for data fetching/caching, mutations, and query invalidation patterns.
UI is built with Tailwind + shadcn/ui components and lucide-react icons; global theme support via next-themes.
Backend is a Motoko canister using Map/List from mo:core for in-canister storage; functions are split by domain (members/diets/workouts/packages/attendance/videos).

Authorization is implemented via a mixin (MixinAuthorization) wrapping an AccessControl module with roles (#admin/#user/#guest) and a one-time admin bootstrap token sourced from an environment variable.
Backend also includes a blob-storage mixin (MixinStorage) providing Caffeine storage gateway authorization + dead-blob pruning utilities.
QR attendance relies on client-side camera scanning (jsQR loaded via CDN) and client-side QR validation (checksum + timestamp freshness) before calling backend to record attendance.
Location-based access control is performed client-side using browser Geolocation (Haversine distance) with gym coordinates/radius fetched from the backend.
Sensitive admin bootstrap token is read from URL hash and cleared, with sessionStorage persistence helpers to reduce leakage risk.
Error handling is emphasized in the frontend via reusable ErrorBoundary components, loading skeletons, and toast notifications (sonner).
Member authentication flow uses a dedicated frontend hook (useMemberAuth) and a backend validateMemberLogin contract that returns structured status compatible with the UI expectations.
known_issues: Member authentication design inconsistency: member mode uses localStorage membershipId with an anonymous actor, but many backend member endpoints (getMemberProfile, QR attendance, video library) require a non-anonymous principal with a user profile linking principal -> memberId (verifyMemberOwnership), so member features may fail without Internet Identity-based member auth.
updateGymLocation(location, radius) ignores the provided location (gymLocation is a constant and the parameter is unused), so gym coordinates cannot be changed at runtime despite the API signature.
updateMember() performs no validation (e.g., duplicate contact info, package/diet/workout existence), allowing inconsistent data compared to createMember() validation rules.
Gym QR code image generation depends on a third-party service (api.qrserver.com); outages or rate limits will break QR display/download.
Admin package creation/edit UI still shows a '$' (USD) currency symbol in the Add Package form despite INR formatting elsewhere.
QR-based attendance check-in/out flow is not working as expected (member scanner/check-in not reflecting changes).
Member login fails immediately after entering Membership ID (on login submit) with "Access denied"—likely backend authorization/principal ownership checks rejecting anonymous member login flow.

existing_features.title: Login selection flow (Admin vs Member)
existing_features.description: Provides a landing experience that lets users choose between Admin login (Internet Identity) and Member login (membership ID), with transition loading states and error boundaries.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication provider
existing_features.description: Implements an InternetIdentityProvider using @dfinity/auth-client with persisted identities, long-lived delegations, login/logout handling, and initialization/error states exposed via a React context and hook.

existing_features.title: Backend actor creation and auth bootstrap
existing_features.description: Creates a backend canister actor configured with the current Internet Identity (or anonymous when not logged in), initializes access control via a secret admin token read from URL hash/session, and triggers React Query invalidation/refetch on identity changes.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control with admin bootstrap token
existing_features.description: Backend authorization layer supporting roles (#admin/#user/#guest). First valid caller can become admin via CAFFEINE_ADMIN_TOKEN; roles can be queried and assigned (admin-only).

existing_features.title: Admin/profile setup and user profile persistence
existing_features.description: Allows authenticated users to store and retrieve a user profile (name/email/memberId optional) and prompts admins to complete a profile if missing.

existing_features.title: Admin dashboard with tabbed management UI
existing_features.description: Admin UI includes Dashboard, Members, Diets, Workouts, Packages, Attendance, and Videos tabs, with statistics cards and production-ready onboarding copy.

existing_features.title: Member directory and member profile management (admin)
existing_features.description: Admins can list/search members and create/edit member profiles including membership dates, status, package assignment, optional diet/workout assignment, and INR pricing fields for discount and final payable amount.

existing_features.title: Member form validation, membership ID generation, and pricing/discount calculation
existing_features.description: Robust member form with safe defaults, custom date pickers, auto-generated membership IDs in create mode, discount input supporting percentage or flat INR, and live price summary (original/discount/final).

existing_features.title: Diet chart CRUD (admin)
existing_features.description: Admins can create and update diet charts with multiple meals (macros and calories), search diets, and view diet summaries in cards.

existing_features.title: Workout chart CRUD (admin)
existing_features.description: Admins can create and update workout charts with multiple exercises (sets/reps/instructions), search workouts, and view workout summaries in cards.

existing_features.title: Membership package management with INR pricing
existing_features.description: Admins can create/edit/delete membership packages (type, duration, priceInRupees), search packages, and view INR-formatted pricing including per-month breakdown.

existing_features.title: Currency utilities for INR formatting
existing_features.description: Formats values as Indian Rupees using en-IN locale, supports bigint/number inputs, per-month computations, and coercion from legacy formats.

existing_features.title: Attendance management (admin manual entry + history)
existing_features.description: Admins can manually check members in/out, view per-member attendance history, and see badges for attendance methods (QR/manual/auto).

existing_features.title: QR attendance system (gym QR generator + member scanner)
existing_features.description: Generates time-based gym QR codes with checksum and periodic refresh; members can scan via camera using jsQR, validate code freshness/checksum, verify GPS location, and then check in/out while polling check-in status.

existing_features.title: Location-based access control (gym radius verification)
existing_features.description: Fetches gym coordinates and allowed radius from backend and verifies a user's proximity using browser geolocation; used to gate QR attendance and member video library access.

existing_features.title: Membership pause/resume controls
existing_features.description: Provides backend endpoints and frontend hooks to pause or resume memberships, with UI confirmations and query invalidation for updated member state.

existing_features.title: Video library with blob storage upload and playback
existing_features.description: Admins can upload workout videos as ExternalBlob with upload progress, store metadata, list and delete videos; videos are rendered with HTML5 playback using blob direct URLs.

existing_features.title: Member-facing dashboard (plans, attendance, QR, and videos)
existing_features.description: Member UI provides a personalized dashboard showing membership status/pricing, assigned diet/workout charts, attendance history, QR attendance card, and a location-gated video library.

existing_features.title: Blob storage operations and maintenance endpoints (backend mixin)
existing_features.description: Includes canister-level utilities for storage authorization, gateway principal refresh, dead blob listing and pruning confirmation, GC triggering, and cashier refill mechanics for cycles management.

existing_features.title: Global error boundaries, loading states, and toast notifications
existing_features.description: Implements reusable error boundaries with reset/reload fallbacks, consistent skeleton loading UIs, and toast notifications for query/mutation feedback.

existing_features.title: Member login validation alignment (frontend/backend)
existing_features.description: Aligns the member login flow so Membership ID validation succeeds end-to-end by updating the backend validateMemberLogin response contract and updating the frontend member auth hook/login UI to match, eliminating false "Invalid ID" failures.

existing_features.title: Member-accessible membership package listing for dashboard display
existing_features.description: Adjusts authorization and/or data-fetching so the member dashboard can fetch package names/prices needed for display without admin-only authorization failures.

existing_features.title: Public backend health-check endpoint
existing_features.description: Adds a public (no-auth) backend query method that returns a simple status payload (e.g., ok/timestamp/version) so the frontend can distinguish connectivity issues from authorization failures.

existing_features.title: Frontend connectivity-aware loading using backend health-check
existing_features.description: Frontend performs a quick backend health-check when stuck in authenticated global loading or when member login hits repeated connection errors; shows a clear message about backend availability and provides a Reload action.

existing_features.title: Resilient canister initialization with optional subsystems
existing_features.description: Backend initialization is hardened so misconfigured optional subsystems (e.g., blob storage integration/env vars) do not prevent the canister from starting; non-dependent endpoints remain available.

existing_features.title: Upgrade-safe persistence via stable state + migration hooks
existing_features.description: Introduces stable state handling and migration hooks to preserve canister data across upgrades and support schema evolution.

existing_features.title: Admin-visible Delete Package action in packages list
existing_features.description: Adds a visible Delete action in the admin packages list with confirmation and live refresh so admins can remove membership packages.

existing_features.title: Update the frontend member login flow to align with membership-ID-only authentication: ensure `useMemberLogin` surfaces backend validation failures as a clear "Invalid or inactive membership ID" message and does not misclassify authorization traps as "Server unavailable". After successful login, member dashboard queries should run with the anonymous actor and must not route the user to the generic Access Denied screen due to backend authorization checks.

Change membership pause to an admin-approved workflow: when a member initiates a pause from their profile, the system must create a pending pause request and must NOT immediately set the member’s membership status to paused until an admin explicitly approves the request.

Frontend (Member): update the member dashboard pause/resume section so that “Pause Membership” becomes a request-based action. The UI must show clear state for: (a) no request (can request pause), (b) pending admin approval (disable re-request and show “Pause request pending approval”), and (c) approved/paused (show resume action as it does today, unless resume is also meant to require approval—keep current resume behavior unless explicitly changed).

Frontend (Admin): add an admin UI surface to review membership pause requests and approve/deny them. This must be accessible from the existing admin dashboard UI (either as a new tab alongside existing tabs or as a clear section within an existing admin tab), and must show at minimum: member ID, member name (if available), request status, request timestamp, and action buttons for approve/deny when pending.

React Query/API integration: update or replace the existing pause membership mutation used by the member dashboard (currently wired to `usePauseMembership`) so it calls the new “request pause” backend endpoint, and add new admin mutations/queries for listing and approving/denying pause requests.

Add an admin notification section that surfaces pause requests awaiting action (approval/rejection) in the admin experience. The notification section must be accessible from the existing admin dashboard UI and must show at minimum the pending request list items with member ID, member name (if available), request timestamp, and quick action buttons to Approve or Deny (for pending only).

Add a clear member-facing status indicator showing the member’s membership status and pause-request status on the member dashboard. The indicator must distinguish at least: (1) Active (no pending request), (2) "Pause request pending approval", and (3) Paused (approved). If a request was denied (including auto-rejected), the UI must show that it was denied and display the admin message when present.

Promote the currently deployed preview build (Version 36) to the live/production deployment so end users see all implemented changes (member pause-request status indicator, admin notifications, pause-request approvals/denials, and auto-expiry behavior).
Perform a production smoke-test pass immediately after publishing and address any release-blocking issues discovered during verification.

new_feature_requests.summary: Proceed with Membership ID–only member login (no Internet Identity): adjust backend/frontend auth so member login and member endpoints work with anonymous principal (or equivalent non-II session), without triggering 'Access denied'.

Update the backend `validateMemberLogin(memberId)` endpoint so membership-ID login works for anonymous callers and never fails with an authorization trap. The endpoint must be callable without Internet Identity and must return `{ isValid: boolean }` with `false` for invalid/inactive memberships (instead of trapping with "Unauthorized" / "Access denied").

Adjust backend authorization for member self-service endpoints to support membership-ID-only sessions (anonymous principal). Any endpoint used by the member dashboard that currently requires `AccessControl.hasPermission(..., #user)` and/or `verifyMemberOwnership(caller, memberId)` must allow access based on the provided `memberId` alone (i.e., treat the membership ID as the authentication factor for member mode). Apply this to member dashboard read endpoints (e.g., member profile, assigned diet/workout, attendance history, current check-in status, gym location read, and video library read) and to member write endpoints (e.g., QR attendance check-in/out, pause/resume membership) while still enforcing that the member exists and (where applicable) the membership is active.
